journal_entry.description: Office relocation services provided to customer, payment due in 10 days
journal_entry.rationale: Atlas Van Lines provided services and earned revenue of $31,500. Since payment is due in 10 days (not received immediately), we debit Accounts Receivable (asset increase) to recognize the customer's obligation. We credit Service Revenue (revenue increase) to recognize the earned income. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly indicates one asset increase line, and the credit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly indicates one revenue increase line. No tax information was provided in the transaction text or tax rules, so no HST/GST lines are added. This follows the accrual basis of accounting under IFRS, recognizing revenue when services are performed regardless of payment timing.

output.reason: Atlas Van Lines provided relocation services to a customer with payment due in 10 days. This is a revenue-generating transaction. The credit side records service revenue = revenue increase (1 line).
